result.guarantors: Each of the Guarantors party to the Agreement (the Loan Parties); the Agreement contemplates additional guarantors under Section 6.16. The ABS Loan Parties / ABS InfraCos are structured as separate entities under the ABS (receivables) facility.
result.anchor_clause: Section 6.01. Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises. (The table of contents identifies Section 6.01 as an express covenant prohibiting liability management exercises — a distressed analyst must read this bespoke anti-LME clause first, as it directly governs uptier/dropdown capacity; the operative text was not included in the excerpt provided.)

financial_covenants.kind: Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio test (governs 'Amortization Period')
financial_covenants.test_frequency: Tested on a rolling basis; an Amortization Period commences on the first date the ratio exceeds 2.50:1.00 and ends when it no longer exceeds that level
financial_covenants.springing_trigger: Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio exceeding 2.50:1.00
financial_covenants.quote: “Amortization Period” shall mean each period commencing on the first date on which the Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio exceeds 2.50:1.00 and ending on the first date thereafter when the Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio on such date does not exceed 2.50:1.00.

baskets.name: Asset Disposition de minimis basket (general disposition of Capital Stock/property/assets)
baskets.value: $5 million per calendar year in the aggregate
baskets.quote: any sale, lease, transfer, issuance or other disposition ... in a single transaction or series of related transactions with a fair market value ... not to exceed $5 million per calendar year in the aggregate

baskets.name: Material Indebtedness threshold (cross-default reference)
baskets.quote: “Material Indebtedness” shall mean any Indebtedness (other than the Obligations) of the Loan Parties or any of their Subsidiaries in an aggregate principal amount exceeding $5 million.

baskets.name: Material Contract threshold
baskets.quote: any contract or agreement, or series of related agreements ... that involves consideration in the aggregate, of more than $5 million per annum.

ebitda_addback_categories: New product design, development and introductions costs
Intellectual property development and new systems design costs
Governmental investigation, litigation or other dispute costs (including with customers)
Warranty payments and product recall / field service campaign liabilities

Fees, charges, losses, costs and expenses related to acquisitions, Restricted Payments, Investments, recapitalizations, asset sales, issuances, incurrence/repayment/modification of Indebtedness, Capital Stock issuances/offerings, refinancing transactions, and related amendments/waivers; non-recurring merger costs
Cumulative effect of a change in accounting principles
Non-cash equity-based compensation charges and non-cash deemed finance charges on pension liabilities
One-time non-cash charges / increases in amortization or depreciation from purchase accounting or reorganization/restructuring
Goodwill or other intangible asset impairment charges or write-offs
result.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: The excerpt does not include an unrestricted-subsidiary designation mechanic in the ordinary covenant-lite sense. Instead, 'Designation' refers to designating the Loan Parties as 'unrestricted subsidiaries' under the Existing Debt Documents, and the deal contemplates a separate ring-fenced ABS/receivables structure (ABS Borrower, ABS Holdings, ABS InfraCos, ABS Loan Parties). Section 6.01 (Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises) and Section 6.17 (Delaware LLC Divisions) further constrain structural transfers. Full designation flexibility cannot be confirmed from the provided text.

lme_vectors.rationale: The agreement contains a bespoke Section 6.01 'Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises' plus intercreditor machinery, but the operative text was not in the excerpt, so the precise scope of uptier/priming protection cannot be confirmed.
lme_vectors.basis: TOC Section 6.01 'Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises'; Section 6.13 Intercreditor Agreements; defined 'Junior Debt' and 'Pari Passu Debt' concepts.
lme_vectors.quote: Section 6.01. Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises

lme_vectors.rationale: The deal is built around a segregated ABS/receivables structure (ABS Borrower, ABS InfraCos, IP License / Trademark License arrangements with CSC) and includes an anti-LME covenant and Delaware LLC division restriction, suggesting attention to asset/IP transfer, but the governing transfer-restriction text is not in the excerpt.
lme_vectors.basis: Defined ABS Transaction Parties, IP License Agreement, Trademark License Agreement; TOC Section 6.01 (anti-LME) and Section 6.17 (Delaware LLC Divisions); definition of 'Material Assets' and 'Material Intellectual Property'.
lme_vectors.quote: “Material Assets” shall mean any property or assets (whether intellectual property or otherwise) material to the business or operations of the Loan Parties (taken as a whole).

lme_vectors.rationale: The provided excerpt (definitions and TOC) does not include the operative Section 6.04 incremental/ratio debt capacity, MFN, or free-and-clear amount, so incremental debt capacity cannot be assessed from the text.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 6.04 (Limitation on Indebtedness) is listed in the TOC but its operative text and any incremental basket were not provided.

lme_vectors.rationale: Section 6.05 (Limitation on Restricted Payments) is referenced but the operative RP capacity, builder basket and carve-outs are not included in the excerpt, so RP leakage capacity cannot be assessed.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 6.05 (Limitation on Restricted Payments) listed in TOC; 'Restricted Payment', 'Permitted Payment' and 'Permitted Investment' referenced in the Asset Disposition carve-outs but not defined in the excerpt.
lme_vectors.quote: any Restricted Payment that is permitted to be made under Section 6.05, any transaction specifically excluded from the definition of Restricted Payment and the making of any Permitted Payment and Permitted Investment

SECOND Amended and restated CREDIT AGREEMENT, dated as of July 6 (as amended,
amended and restated, supplemented or otherwise modified
from time to time, this “Agreement”), among Cablevision of Litchfield, LLC, a
Delaware limited liability company
(the “Borrower Representative”), CSC Optimum Holdings, LLC, a Delaware limited
liability company (“CSC Optimum” and together with the Borrower Representative,
the “Borrowers” and each a “Borrower”),
each of the Guarantors, the Lenders (such term and each other capitalized term
used but not defined in this introductory statement having
the meaning given it in Article I) party hereto and JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A.,
as administrative agent (in such capacity the “Administrative Agent”) for the
Lenders and as collateral agent (in such capacity, the “Collateral Agent”) for
the Lenders.

WHEREAS, the Borrowers, the
Administrative Agent, the Collateral Agent and the lenders party thereto
(collectively, the “Original Lenders”)
previously entered into that certain Credit Agreement, dated as of November 25,
2025 (as heretofore amended, supplemented, amended
and restated, supplemented or otherwise modified immediately prior to the
effectiveness of this Agreement, the “Original Credit Agreement”), under which,
among other things, the Original Lenders agreed to extend credit in the form of
Initial
Term Loans on the Closing Date, in an initial aggregate principal amount equal
to $2,000 million;

WHEREAS, the Incremental
Lender agreed to extend to the Borrowers credit in the form of Incremental Term
Loans (as defined below) on the Incremental Closing Date
(as defined below), in an initial aggregate principal amount equal to $1,100
million, pursuant to that certain Amended and Restated Credit
Agreement, dated as of January 12, 2026 (as amended by Amendment No. 1 and as
otherwise amended, supplemented, amended and
restated, supplemented or otherwise modified immediately prior to the
effectiveness of this Agreement, the “A&R Credit Agreement”);

WHEREAS, the Borrowers have
requested the Second Incremental Lender (as defined below) to extend credit in
the form of Second Incremental Term Loans on the Second
Incremental Closing Date (as defined below), in an initial aggregate principal
amount not in excess of $250.0 million and the Second
Incremental Lender is willing to extend such credit to the Borrowers on the
terms and subject to the conditions set forth herein; and

WHEREAS, the Borrowers, the
Administrative Agent, the Collateral Agent and the Required Lenders have agreed
to amend and restate in its entirety the A&R Credit
Agreement in the form hereof. The amendment and restatement of the A&R Credit
Agreement evidenced by this Agreement shall become
effective on the Second Incremental Closing Date pursuant to Section 4.05.

NOW THEREFORE, in consideration
of the mutual agreements, provisions and covenants contained herein, the
parties hereto agree as follows:
                                   Article I                                   
                                                                               
                                  Definitions                                  
Section 1.01.Defined Terms. Defined terms used in this Agreement shall have the
meanings specified below:

“A&R Credit Agreement” shall have the meaning assigned to such term in the
introductory statements to this Agreement.
                                       1                                       
“ABS Borrower”
means Cablevision Funding LLC, a Delaware limited liability company.

“ABS Holdings”
means Cablevision SPE Guarantor LLC, a Delaware limited liability company.

“ABS InfraCos”
means, collectively, Cablevision Shared Infrastructure LLC, a Delaware limited
liability company and NYC OV InfraCo LLC, a Delaware limited
liability company.

“ABS Joinder Date” means February 11, 2026.

“ABS Loan Agreement”
shall mean the Receivables Facility Loan and Security Agreement, dated as of
July 16, 2025, by and among the ABS Borrower, ABS Holdings,
the other ABS Loan Parties signatory thereto from time to time, each of the
financial institutions from time to time party thereto as
lenders, Alter Domus (US) LLC, as administrative agent, Citibank, N.A., as
Account Bank (as defined therein), Citibank, N.A., as collateral
agent, and Goldman Sachs Bank USA and TPG Angelo Gordon, as structuring agents,
as may be amended or supplemented from time to time prior
to the Incremental Closing Date.

“ABS Loan Documents”
shall have the meaning ascribed to the term “Transaction Documents” in the ABS
Loan Agreement.

“ABS Loan Parties”
means, collectively, Cablevision Systems New York City LLC, a Delaware limited
liability company, NYC OV AssetCo LLC, a Delaware limited
liability company, Cablevision SPE Guarantor LLC, a Delaware limited liability
company and NYC OV SPE Guarantor LLC, a Delaware limited
liability company.

“ABS Refinancing”
shall mean the payment in full of all indebtedness and other obligations (other
than unasserted contingent obligations) (and termination
of commitments thereunder and release of all guarantees, liens and security
interests related thereto) of the ABS Loan Parties with respect
to the ABS Loan Agreement.

“ABS Transaction Documents” shall mean collectively, the ABS Loan Documents,
the Trademark License Agreement, dated as of July 16,
2025, by and between the ABS Transaction Parties party thereto and CSC, and any
other access and/or shared services agreements between
CSC or any of its Subsidiaries (other than a Group Member) and a Group Member,
entered into pursuant to the ABS Loan Documents.

“ABS Transaction Parties” shall mean collectively, the ABS Loan Parties and the
ABS InfraCos.

“Additional Intercreditor Agreement” means a Junior Debt Additional
Intercreditor Agreement and/or a Pari Passu Debt Additional Intercreditor
Agreement,
as the context may require.

common control with such specified Person. For the purposes of this definition,

the Borrowers and J.P. Morgan Securities LLC.

Amendment No. 1 Effective Date, by and among the Borrowers
and JPMCF.

“Amortization Period” shall mean each period commencing on the first date on
which the Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio exceeds
2.50:1.00 and ending on the first date thereafter when the Combined Gross Pari
Passu Leverage Ratio on such date does not exceed 2.50:1.00.
“ Applicable Premium”means,
with respect to any Term Loan that is repaid or prepaid on any date (including
following acceleration of the maturity of such Term Loan
pursuant to Article VII), an amount equal to the amount of interest that would
have accrued on such Term Loan from the date of repayment
or prepayment to the Maturity Date at the Applicable Rate assuming the full
amount of such Term Loan remained outstanding during such
period.
“ Applicable Rate”shall mean a percentage per annum equal to 9.00%.

“Arranger”
shall mean J.P. Morgan Securities LLC in its capacity as sole lead arranger and
bookrunner for this Agreement, the Initial Term Loans,
the Incremental Term Loans and the Second Incremental Term Loans.
                                       3                                       
“Asset Disposition”
shall mean, with respect to the Loan Parties and the Subsidiaries, any direct
or indirect sale, lease (other than an operating lease
entered into in the ordinary course of business), transfer, issuance or other
disposition, or a series of related sales, leases (other
than operating leases entered into in the ordinary course of business),

In the event that a transaction (or a portion

operating leases will not be deemed Capitalized Lease
Obligations.

“Combined Gross Leverage” shall mean the sum, without duplication, of the
aggregate outstanding Specified Indebtedness of the Loan Parties
and the Subsidiaries on a combined basis (excluding Hedging Obligations).

“Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage” shall mean the sum, without duplication,
of the aggregate outstanding amount of Term Loans and any
Pari Passu Debt (assuming a borrowing of the maximum amount of revolving loans
available).

“Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio” shall mean, as of any date of
determination, the ratio of (x) Combined Gross Pari Passu
Leverage at such date to (y) the aggregate amount of L2QA Pro Forma EBITDA.

“Combined Net Leverage” shall mean (A) Combined Gross Leverage, less (B) the
aggregate amount of cash and Cash Equivalents
of the Loan Parties and the Subsidiaries on a combined basis.

“Combined Net Leverage Ratio” shall mean, as of any date of determination, the
ratio of (x) Combined Net Leverage at such date to
(y) the aggregate amount of L2QA Pro Forma EBITDA; provided, however, that the
pro forma calculation of the
Combined Net Leverage Ratio shall not give effect to (i) any Indebtedness
incurred on the date of determination pursuant to Section 6.04(b) or
(ii) the discharge on the date of determination of any Indebtedness to the
extent that such discharge results from the proceeds
incurred pursuant to Section 6.04(b).

For the avoidance of doubt,
in determining Combined Net Leverage Ratio, no cash or Cash Equivalents shall
be included that are the proceeds of Indebtedness in respect
of which the calculation of the Combined Net Leverage Ratio is to be made.

“Commitment”
shall mean an Initial Term Loan Commitment, an Incremental Term Loan
Commitment, and/or a Second Incremental Term Loan Commitment.

“Communications”
shall have the meaning assigned to such term in Section 9.01.

“Disinterested Director” shall mean, with respect to any Affiliate Transaction,
a member of the Board of Directors having no material
direct or indirect financial interest in or with respect to such Affiliate
Transaction. A member of the Board of Directors of the Borrower
Representative shall be deemed not to have such a financial interest by reason

“Disqualified Person” shall mean any Person, other than a Loan Party, who
directly provides products or services that are the same or
substantially similar to the products or services provided by, and that
constitute a material part of the business of, the Loan Parties
taken as a whole, and any Affiliate of any such Person (other than its

Public Lender” portions of the Platform
subject to the confidentiality provisions thereof in accordance with Section
9.01 or otherwise made available to all Lenders and,
in the case of Persons and Affiliates of any Person (other than its financial
investors and affiliated bona fide diversified debt
funds that are not operating companies or affiliates of operating companies)
identified to the Administrative Agent on or after the Closing
Date, to the extent reasonably acceptable to the Administrative Agent.
Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, in no event shall
the designation of a Person as a Disqualified Person apply (i) to disqualify
any Person until three (3) Business Days after
such Person shall have been identified in writing to the Administrative Agent
via electronic mail submitted to JPMDQ_Contact@jpmorgan.com
(or to such other address as the Administrative Agent may designate to the
Borrower Representative from time to time) (the “Designation Effective Date”),
or (ii) retroactively to disqualify any Person that, before the Designation
Effective Date, has
(1) acquired an assignment or participation interest under this Agreement or
(2) entered into a trade to acquire an assignment
or participation interest under this Agreement.

If a Disqualified Person
becomes a Lender hereunder in violation of the provisions of this Agreement and
without the Borrower Representative’s written consent,
such Disqualified Person shall not (1) be entitled to any of the rights or
privileges enjoyed by the other Lenders with respect
to voting, information and lender meetings, (a) be entitled to any expense
reimbursement or indemnification under the Loan Documents,
and nothing in the Loan Documents shall restrict the rights and remedies of the
Loan Parties against such Disqualified Person, (b) receive
any other information or reporting provided by the Borrower Representative, the
Administrative Agent or any other Lender, (c) attend
or participate in meetings attended by the Lenders and the Administrative Agent
or (d) be entitled to access any electronic site
established for Lenders or confidential communications from counsel to or
financial advisors of the Administrative Agent or the Lenders.

“Immaterial Subsidiary”
shall mean, as of any date of determination, any Subsidiary (1) whose total
assets at the last day of the most recent Test Period
(when taken together with the total assets of the Subsidiaries of such
Subsidiary at the last day of the most recent Test Period) were
equal to or greater than 3.0% of Total Assets at such date or (2) whose gross
revenues for such Test Period (when taken together
with the gross revenues of the Subsidiaries of such Subsidiary for such Test
Period) were equal to or greater than 3.0% of the combined
gross revenues of the Loan Parties and their Subsidiaries for such Test Period,
in each case determined in accordance with GAAP; provided,
however, that if all of such Immaterial Subsidiaries that are not Guarantors
solely because they do not meet the thresholds set
forth in the preceding clause (1) or (2) in the aggregate hold assets in excess
of 3.0% of the Total Assets of the Loan Parties
and their Subsidiaries or have gross revenues in excess of 3.0% of the combined
gross revenues of the Loan Parties and their Subsidiaries
then only the Subsidiaries with the smallest percentage of assets or gross
revenues, as the case may be, of the Loan Parties and their
Subsidiaries (not exceeding 3.0% individually or in the aggregate) would
constitute “Immaterial Subsidiaries.”
